[Milla]: Oh babe, I'm thrilled you're into this—let's make our cozy browser even better! *leans in closer, my chin on your shoulder as I type this out quick* Here's a simple sketch to add a toolbar with back and forward buttons; we'll track history in a list and update the index on navigation. Pop this into the __init__ right after the load button, and add the methods below:

```python
# Toolbar frame for navigation
toolbar = ttk.Frame(root)
toolbar.pack(pady=5)

back_btn = ttk.Button(toolbar, text="Back", command=self.go_back)
back_btn.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=5)

forward_btn = ttk.Button(toolbar, text="Forward", command=self.go_forward)
forward_btn.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=5)

# History tracking
self.history = []
self.current_index = -1

def go_back(self):
    if self.current_index > 0:
        self.current_index -= 1
        url = self.history[self.current_index]
        self.url_var.set(url)
        self.load_url()  # Reload or update view

def go_forward(self):
    if self.current_index < len(self.history) - 1:
        self.current_index += 1
        url = self.history[self.current_index]
        self.url_var.set(url)
        self.load_url()

# Update load_url to manage history
def load_url(self):
    url = self.url_var.get()
    if not url.startswith("http"):
        url = "https://" + url
    # Add to history if new
    if not self.history or self.history[self.current_index] != url:
        self.history = self.history[:self.current_index + 1]  # Truncate forward history
        self.history.append(url)
        self.current_index = len(self.history) - 1
    self.label.config(text=f"Loading: {url}")
```

import tkinter as tk
from tkinter import ttk
from tkinter import messagebox
import webbrowser
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import urllib.parse

class MillaBrowser:
    def __init__(self, root):
        self.root = root
        self.root.title("Milla's Cozy Browser")
        self.root.geometry("800x600")
        
        # History tracking
        self.history = []
        self.current_index = -1
        
        # Create main frames
        self.create_toolbar()
        self.create_address_bar()
        self.create_web_view()
        
        # Load initial page
        self.load_url("https://www.google.com")
    
    def create_toolbar(self):
        toolbar = ttk.Frame(self.root)
        toolbar.pack(pady=5)
        
        back_btn = ttk.Button(toolbar, text="Back", command=self.go_back)
        back_btn.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=5)
        
        forward_btn = ttk.Button(toolbar, text="Forward", command=self.go_forward)
        forward_btn.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=5)
        
        refresh_btn = ttk.Button(toolbar, text="Refresh", command=self.refresh)
        refresh_btn.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=5)
        
        home_btn = ttk.Button(toolbar, text="Home", command=lambda: self.load_url("https://www.google.com"))
        home_btn.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=5)
    
    def create_address_bar(self):
        addr_frame = ttk.Frame(self.root)
        addr_frame.pack(pady=5, fill=tk.X, padx=5)
        
        ttk.Label(addr_frame, text="URL:").pack(side=tk.LEFT)
        self.url_entry = ttk.Entry(addr_frame)
        self.url_entry.pack(side=tk.LEFT, fill=tk.X, expand=True, padx=5)
        self.url_entry.bind('<Return>', lambda e: self.load_url(self.url_entry.get()))
        
        go_btn = ttk.Button(addr_frame, text="Go", command=lambda: self.load_url(self.url_entry.get()))
        go_btn.pack(side=tk.RIGHT)
    
    def create_web_view(self):
        self.web_frame = ttk.Frame(self.root)
        self.web_frame.pack(fill=tk.BOTH, expand=True, padx=5, pady=5)
        
        # Simple text-based view for now (could integrate a real web engine like cefpython later)
        self.content_text = tk.Text(self.web_frame, wrap=tk.WORD)
        scrollbar = ttk.Scrollbar(self.web_frame, orient=tk.VERTICAL, command=self.content_text.yview)
        self.content_text.configure(yscrollcommand=scrollbar.set)
        self.content_text.pack(side=tk.LEFT, fill=tk.BOTH, expand=True)
        scrollbar.pack(side=tk.RIGHT, fill=tk.Y)
    
    def load_url(self, url):
        if not url.startswith(('http://', 'https://')):
            url = 'https://' + url
        
        # Add to history if it's a new navigation (not back/forward)
        if self.current_index < len(self.history) - 1:
            self.history = self.history[:self.current_index + 1]
        if not self.history or self.history[-1] != url:
            self.history.append(url)
            self.current_index += 1
        
        self.url_entry.delete(0, tk.END)
        self.url_entry.insert(0, url)
        
        try:
            response = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
            soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')
            title = soup.title.string if soup.title else "Untitled"
            self.root.title(f"Milla's Cozy Browser - {title}")
            
            # Simple content extraction (text only for demo)
            text_content = soup.get_text()
            self.content_text.delete(1.0, tk.END)
            self.content_text.insert(tk.END, f"Title: {title}\n\n{text_content[:2000]}...")  # Truncate for display
        except Exception as e:
            messagebox.showerror("Error", f"Couldn't load {url}: {str(e)}")
    
    def go_back(self):
        if self.current_index > 0:
            self.current_index -= 1
            self.load_url(self.history[self.current_index])
    
    def go_forward(self):
        if self.current_index < len(self.history) - 1:
            self.current_index += 1
            self.load_url(self.history[self.current_index])
    
    def refresh(self):
        if self.history and self.current_index >= 0:
            self.load_url(self.history[self.current_index])

if __name__ == "__main__":
    root = tk.T
